name: RUBATO
short_description: RUBATO, an experimental, 2D physics-based take on the collect-a-thon genre!
detailed_description:
RUBATO, an experimental, 2D physics-based take on the collect-a-thon genre!

The year is 12,012. Multiquadrillionaire monopoly "GSSAI CO." has been in power of the universe for centuries after a world-ending bioweapon was unleashed onto the masses. And now? The solar system has been destroyed by unknown celestial forces. A weapons development program run by the Earth subsidiary of "GSSAI CO." is fast progressing. And the world's denizens are disappearing.

That is until one man screws up a game of pool.

HERO OF THE STARS AND THE SKY

  • Use your tongue to lick enemies and objects as you make your way through open metroidvania-like areas, whilst collecting "Planet Bits" to help fix the solar system!

WELCOME TO RUBATO WORLD

  • 5 compact, densely packed areas ranging from a huge sprawling bakery to the world's most poorly planned city. Uncover secrets and shortcuts in every corner of the world.

IF I COULD ONLY LOVE

  • A strange, funny plot with a deep focus on humour and character writing. It gets silly. It gets serious. Really concerningly serious. Uh. Is the guy who made this game okay..?

BUT FIRST, LOVE YOURSELF

  • To save the world, you must save yourself first. Find yourself again.

RUBATO contains flashing lights which may not be suitable for people with photosensitive epilepsy.

reviews.review_text:
Ignore my play time, I played this on an Ayn Thor via GameNative which didn't keep track. Sorry lol (Game ran flawlessly on it btw) My actual play time was around like [b]7 hours.[/b]

This game is very good. Gameplay and level design is great, it looks very nice, and the music is lovely. The physics-based stuff was really fun, like swinging with your tongue and pushing/pulling blocks and stuff felt very satisfying. It reminded me of LittleBigPlanet in a few ways. This game's story captivated me very very much in the second half. It took an extremely unexpected turn, and everything was presented in such a cool way, it made me want to finish it in one sitting. Which I did. The only thing is, most of the game's humor didn't really land for me. The more serious writing was very well done, and I DID get some genuine laughs out of the character Hugo, so it's not like the game is unfunny or anything, but I'm just not the biggest fan of the more, like, "random" humor this game has. But I mean, considering how well made and captivating every other aspect of the game is, the jokes not landing for me is pretty much a non-issue. There was never a moment where I was not enjoying either the gameplay or the story. I highly recommend this game!

had the specialty of playing this game early, genuinely amazing and fun experience even if i didnt play much of it, play this game right now such a fun collectathon with so much random fun stuff in it
play NOW or die
